title: Strategic Advisor - Chief People and Culture Officer (12-month contract)
description: THE OPPORTUNITY Act as the brain trust for the Chief People and Culture Officer (CPCO) on all matters related to people strategy. Take time to understand the work of the Foundation through the senior business partners. Provide strategic advice, insights, and support regarding ongoing enterprise initiatives . Review functional-related plans with the Leadership Team before execution. Collaborate with CPCO to develop or redesign P&C goals and objectives. WAYS YOU CAN CONTRIBUTE Provide strategic insights and recommendations to support the CPCO in decision- making and executive engagements. Review ongoing strategic plans. Provide recommendations on proposed HR initiatives. Prioritize the P&C roadmap to determine sequencing and pacing. Hold collaborative meetings with P&C Leadership teams. Oversee the consolidation and quality of Board and EXCO presentations. Work with the CPCO on all Board related presentation preparations. P artner with the P&C Leadership Team to prepare EXCO submissions, including consolidation and advisory on strategic positioning. Review and refine strategy documents across the talent value chain to ensure alignment with the Foundation’s mission. Review the P&C modernization strategy with internal teams and the Deloitte Canada working group. Propose overarching goals, objectives , and priorities for 2025–2026. Collaborate with the P&C Leadership Team to develop strategic initiatives. Actively listen to team perspectives and ideas in alignment with organizational values. Ask thought-provoking questions to ensure initiatives are tied to P&C strategy, outcome driven , and measurable. Meet with P&C Leads to provide thought partnership, insights, and guidance to inform strategic choices. Support the P&C Leadership in prioritizing key People and Culture projects and initiatives. Lead conversations with the P &C LT on initiative importance, project impact, resource needs, dependencies, and timing. Monitor progress of strategic projects to ensure alignment with enterprise initiatives. Ensure each strategic project has clearly defined success measures. Define criteria for integrating P&C projects with enterprise initiatives. Gather relevant project information and ensure goal clarity. Suggest success criteria for assessing program outcomes. Critique the People and Culture priorities central dashboard. Review the P&C Dashboard from the reporting team. Analyze critical performance areas, monitor progress, and guide achievement. Plan and coordinate Leadership Team connects and retreats. Develop P&C agendas and manage pre- reads . Capture key action points from meetings. Lead retreat planning and agenda development. Partner with external consultants to ensure delivery of P&C Leadership and extended Leadership retreats. Develop themes, agendas, and session plans. Support the Leadership Team in identifying strategic objectives for sessions and tailoring content to the audience. Design interactive session activities to encourage engagement. Create schedules aligned with the overall strategic plan. Oversee logistical coordination for productive Leadership Team engagements. Project manages P&C Leadership meetings, retreats, and events. Oversee planning activities with the CPCO, Executive Assistant and P&C coordinators. WHO YOU ARE Master’s degree or equivalent. At least 15 years’ experience in a strategic advisory capacity. Experience in People Leadership at an executive level. Executive communication skills. Technical competence in People & Culture systems and processes. Flexible, adaptable, and able to execute a range of job duties and changing priorities. Professional maturity, sensitivity to different cultures , and impeccable integrity that exemplifies the Foundation’s values. Possess excellent verbal, written, and presentation skills with the ability to articulate information to various constituents across cultures. You have committed to Mastercard Foundation’s values and vision . The Mastercard Foundation values the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the organization and we are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. Completion of satisfactory business references and background checks are essential conditions of employment. For more information and to sign up for the Foundation’s newsletter, please visit http://www.mastercardfdn.org/ Follow the Foundation on Twitter at MastercardFdn

title: Director Gender
description: THE OPPORTUNITY This role is responsible for building Gender thematic area within Pan-African Programs by setting strategic direction and engaging partners throughout program design and implementation and focused on the Foundation’s commitment to enabling dignified and fulfilling work for young women and young people in general. The role is responsible for providing thematic expertise and facilitating understanding of gender dynamics and supporting establishment of responsive programmatic interventions at all levels of the Foundation WAYS YOU CAN CONTRIBUTE Drive Gender strategy and roadmap development in collaboration with Foundation executive leadership. Work with Foundation leaders and thematic resources to articulate and update the Gender strategy and Roadmap. Provide oversight on Gender programs during the various program lifecycle stages, including scoping, incubation, design, and delivery. Engage and maintain strong strategic relationships with relevant stakeholders, including institutions of interest, peer funders, and others relevant to the Foundation’s work in the field of Gender. Convene thematic expertise and advance thought leadership externally and across the Foundation – at Pan African, E&T, HECIS, and Country Program level. Cultivate relationships and facilitate engagements with thought leaders and other influencers to advance the Foundations thought leadership aligned to Gender. Interface with Foundation business partners across other functional areas to test/refine and deploy thematic area operating models. Lead the team in embedding operational excellence in day-to-day prioritization of work within Gender, team resourcing, delegation of tasks to meet partner demands, performance management input and other people management processes. Interface with Country Directors in the design and delivery of Country Programs to ensure strategic cohesion, coordination of programming efforts, and knowledge sharing. Champion the integration of gender considerations across the Foundation and specifically in Country, Human Capital Development and Pan African Program portfolio design. Lead relevant internal and technical expert teams to advance the Foundation’s overall Gender outcomes in all areas of the Foundation’s work. Collaborate with leadership teams across the Foundation to co-design programs, embed designs, adequate resources and budgets to ensure implementation of safeguarding approaches and mechanisms. Oversee the utilization of the Foundation’s Safeguarding resources and teams to strategically and practically implement policies, procedures and guidelines that meaningfully integrate safeguarding measures across the Foundation. WHO YOU ARE Master’s degree in Social Sciences, including gender/women’s/youth studies, education-related field from a recognized university. Relevant professional qualification. Member of a relevant professional body. Minimum of ten (15) years’ relevant experience in a similar organization setting, with seven (7) years at a senior management level in a Pan African role or Global role. Experience and knowledge of/with the landscape of local, national, regional, and pan-African women’s and youth-serving organizations and networks, as well as knowledge of the country-specific social, economic and political contexts. Experience in partnership management, including capacity building, coordination and networking for effective gender mainstreaming and youth empowerment. Extensive experience in gender and youth and women-centred programme design and implementation in Africa. Experience in leading organizational policy development and delivery on safeguarding and ensuring its effective delivery across a global organization. Experience developing training and facilitating capacity building initiatives on gender, youth and women empowerment and safeguarding. Ability to be flexible within a fast-paced, changing environment Demonstrable influencing skills at a senior leadership level and with high-profile stakeholders. Proven success in developing and leading a global team. Strong interpersonal and influencing skills, able to manage conflict and handle sensitive issues, particularly in cross-cultural and cross-departmental contexts. Positive, solution-focused attitude. Proven people management skills and team-building skills. Experience in the design and analysis of monitoring, evaluation and research. Excellent interpersonal skills and experience working in a multi-cultural team. Excellent written, presentation and communication skills. High attention to detail and the ability to work under pressure and tight deadlines. The Mastercard Foundation values the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the organization and we are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. Completion of satisfactory business references and background checks are essential conditions of employment. For more information and to sign up for the Foundation’s newsletter, please visit http://www.mastercardfdn.org/ Follow the Foundation on Twitter at MastercardFdn
